name: 12th Lord Bhukti — Emigration & Foreign Residence
description: The sub-period of the 12th house lord acts as a primary timing window for foreign travel, emigration, and change of residence.

source_text.work_name: Predictive Astrology Through The Nirayana System
source_text.quote: 12th-from-4th: Change of Residence / Emigration... Bhukti of the 12th lord will provide foreign travel or foreign residence.

all_of: The Antardasha (Bhukti) of the 12th Lord is active

interpretation.meaning: The sub-period of the 12th house lord acts as a primary timing window for foreign travel, emigration, and change of residence. It triggers a physical separation from the homeland (4th house) as the 12th house is 9th from the 4th (long journeys from home) and 12th from the 1st (isolation/foreign environment).

metadata.notes: A fundamental rule linking the 12th house activation directly to foreign settlement.

name: Jupiter-Rahu Dasha — Journeys to Distant Lands
description: When Rahu is strong and well-placed, its sub-period within Jupiter's major period activates beneficial long-distance journeys and temporary foreign residence.

source_text.quote: Effects like attachment to yoga, gain of wealth and grains during the first five months... meeting with a foreign king (high dignitary)... journeys to distant lands, bathing in holy places, will be derived in the Antardasa of Rahu in the Dasa of Jupiter, if Rahu be in his sign of exaltation, in his own sign, in his moolatrikona or be in kendra or trikona from the Ascendant or be aspected by the lord of kendra or be associated with or aspected by a benefic.

any_of: Rahu is in its sign of exaltation
Rahu is in its own sign
Rahu is in a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from the Ascendant
Rahu is in a Trikona (5th, 9th) from the Ascendant
Rahu is aspected by the lord of a Kendra
Rahu is associated with or aspected by a benefic planet

interpretation.meaning: When Rahu (significator of foreign elements) is strong and well-placed, its sub-period within Jupiter's major period activates highly beneficial long-distance journeys, temporary foreign residence, and interactions with foreign authorities.

metadata.notes: Rahu naturally rules foreign lands and distant travel.

name: Rahu-Sun Dasha — Foreign Recognition & Settlement
description: Rahu (foreign connections) and Sun (authority/fame), when placed harmoniously, create a successful window for settling abroad with professional recognition.

source_text.quote: Well being in every way and recognition from the kings (high dignitaries) in foreign countries, will be the results, if the Sun be in a kendra, trikona, the 3rd or the 11th from the lord of the Dasa (Rahu).

any_of: The Sun is positioned in a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) from Rahu
The Sun is positioned in a Trikona (5th, 9th) from Rahu
The Sun is positioned in the 3rd house from Rahu
The Sun is positioned in the 11th house from Rahu

interpretation.meaning: The combination of Rahu (foreign connections) and the Sun (authority/fame), when placed harmoniously relative to each other, creates a successful window for settling abroad with high professional recognition and status.

metadata.notes: Highlights the importance of mutual planetary placement (Dasha Pravesh relative geometry) for favorable foreign relocation.

name: Venus-Moon Dasha (Afflicted Moon) — Forced Homeland Separation
description: An afflicted Moon during Venus's period triggers forced or distressing relocation, separation from homeland and family.

source_text.quote: ...journeys to foreign lands or pilgrimage, distress to wife and children and separation from kinsmen, will be the results if the Moon be in her sign of debilitation, combust or be in the 6th, the 8th or the 12th from the Ascendant or the lord of the Dasa (Venus).

any_of: The Moon is in its sign of debilitation (Scorpio)
The Moon is positioned in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Ascendant
The Moon is positioned in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from Venus

interpretation.meaning: An afflicted Moon during Venus's period triggers forced or distressing relocation. It signifies losing comfort, separating from the homeland and family, and wandering abroad due to difficult circumstances.

metadata.notes: Indicates a challenging relocation rather than a planned, successful one.

name: Sun-Jupiter Dasha — Prosperous Foreign Residence
description: Sun (authority) and Jupiter (expansion/dharma) create a highly favorable period for establishing respected residence in a foreign country.

source_text.quote: During the sub-period of Jupiter in Sun's dasa, one will be virtuous (or he will be liberal in donating) and enjoy all luxuries. He will be rich in knowledge and possess wealth, acquire fame and live in foreign countries.

interpretation.meaning: The interaction of the Sun (authority) and Jupiter (expansion/dharma) creates a highly favorable period for establishing a successful, comfortable, and respected residence in a foreign country.

metadata.notes: Supports moving abroad for higher education, religious purposes, or high-status career postings.

name: Rahu-Ketu Dasha — Sudden Foreign Journey
description: The nodal axis (Rahu-Ketu) inherently represents breaking boundaries. Their mutual Dasha period strongly triggers sudden foreign travel.

source_text.quote: During the Antardasa of Ketu in the Dasha of Rahu, there will be journeys to foreign countries, danger from the king (government), rheumatic fever etc., and loss of cattle.

interpretation.meaning: The nodal axis (Rahu-Ketu) inherently represents breaking boundaries and foreign environments. Their mutual Dasha period strongly triggers sudden, often chaotic or required travel to foreign countries, accompanied by instability.

metadata.notes: Highly reliable indicator of geographical movement, though often marked by physical or legal friction.

name: Jupiter-Mercury Dasha (Afflicted) — Foreign Wanderings & Instability
description: When Jupiter and Mercury are badly placed relative to each other, it results in stressful, aimless wandering in foreign lands.

source_text.quote: There will be loss of wealth, journeys to foreign countries, danger from thieves, while travelling, wounds, burning sensations, eye troubles, wanderings in foreign lands, if Mercury be in the 6th, the 8th or the 12th from the lord of Dasa (Jupiter) or be associated with a malefic without the aspect of a benefice.

any_of: Mercury is positioned in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house relative to Jupiter
Mercury is associated with a malefic planet and receives no benefic aspect

interpretation.meaning: When the planets of growth (Jupiter) and movement/commerce (Mercury) are badly placed relative to each other (Shadashtaka/Dwidwadasa), it results in stressful, aimless wandering in foreign lands and loss of stability.

metadata.notes: Indicates unstable relocation where the native struggles to settle in the new country.

name: Rasi Dasha with Nodes in Trikona — Exile to Foreign Country
description: In sign-based Dasha systems, when nodes influence the active sign through trinal aspect, it forces the native out of homeland.

source_text.quote: The Dasa of the rasi will cause distress to children, exile to a foreign country and continuous disturbances in his life if Rahu and Ketu be in the trikona to the rasi.

all_of: A Rasi Dasha (sign-based period like Chara Dasha) is active
any_of: Rahu is positioned in a Trikona (5th, 9th) from the active Dasha sign
Ketu is positioned in a Trikona (5th, 9th) from the active Dasha sign

interpretation.meaning: In sign-based Dasha systems, when the nodes of disruption and foreign elements (Rahu/Ketu) influence the active sign through a trinal aspect, it forces the native out of their homeland, often manifesting as exile or sudden required relocation.

metadata.notes: Applies strictly to Jaimini or other sign-based Dasha calculations, rather than Vimshottari.
